title: Writing Conditions
learningGoal: The learner will synthesize conditional sentences, correctly choosing between the '~to' form for mechanics and the '~tara' form for general hypotheticals.
instructions: Write four conditional sentences in Japanese based on the English prompts. Decide carefully whether to use the '~to' form or the '~tara' form. Remember: Nouns and na-adjectives take 'dattara'.

contentItems.text: For sentence 1, use the '~to' form because it describes a mechanical consequence.

writingPrompt: Translate: 1) If you push the button, the door opens. 2) If it rains, I will not go. 3) If it is cheap, I will buy it. 4) If you are free, please come.
promptGoal: To construct grammatically accurate conditionals differentiating between mechanical rules (~to) and hypothetical situations (~tara) across verbs and adjectives.
referenceHints: Sentence 1: [Button] wo [Push-Dictionary form] to, doa ga [Opens].
Sentence 2: [Rain falls-Ta form] ra, [Will not go].

modelExampleOutputs.targetText: ボタンを おすと、ドアが あきます。あめが ふったら、いきません。やすかったら、かいます。ひまだったら、きて ください。
modelExampleOutputs.transliteration: Botan wo osu to, doa ga akimasu. Ame ga futtara, ikimasen. Yasukattara, kaimasu. Hima dattara, kite kudasai.
modelExampleOutputs.baseTranslation: If you push the button, the door opens. If it rains, I will not go. If it is cheap, I will buy it. If you are free, please come.
modelExampleOutputs.annotation: Perfect selection of conditionals: ~to for the mechanical action (osu to), ~tara for verbs (futtara), i-adjectives (yasukattara), and na-adjectives (hima dattara).

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: ボタンを おすと、ドアが あきます。あめが ふったら、いきません。やすかったら、かいます。ひまだったら、きて ください。
acceptableAlternates: ボタンを おすと ドアが あきます。あめが ふったら いきません。やすかったら かいます。ひまだったら きて ください。
adminCorrectAnswerSet.grammarBaseline: Mastery of the morphological changes required to produce past-conditional forms across verb and adjective classes.

feedbackRules.onPass: Brilliant writing! You correctly applied all four variations of the conditional forms.
feedbackRules.onFail: Review the rule for na-adjectives. 'Hima' requires 'dattara' to become conditional.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good try! Make sure you didn't write 'Oshitara' for the first sentence. While understandable, '~to' is the most natural for machines.

hintText: To make 'Yasui' (cheap) conditional, drop the 'i' and add 'kattara'.
